title: Smoky Fermented Algerian Beef Tagine
description: A velvety, fermented beef tagine that captures the monsoon heat of Algeria with smoky bay leaves, fermented tomato paste, and a subtle hint of smoke-completely sugarfree.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a heavy‑bottomed tagine or Dutch oven over medium‑high heat.
Add the beef cubes and brown on all sides until a deep brown crust forms, about 5–7 minutes.
Stir in the sliced onion, smashed garlic, bay leaves, smoked paprika, smoked salt, and pepper; cook for another 3 minutes until fragrant.
Pour in the fermented tomato paste, red wine, and water; stir to combine and loosen any browned bits from the bottom.
Add the rosemary sprigs, reduce the heat to low, cover, and let the tagine simmer for 1¾–2 hours, or until the beef is fork‑tender.
If available, infuse a small amount of dry wood chips (e.g., oak or hickory) into a smoking box or use a stovetop smoker to add a subtle smoky aroma during the last 15 minutes of cooking.
Check seasoning and adjust with additional smoked salt or pepper if needed.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh herbs or a squeeze of lemon, with a side of crusty bread or a low‑carb grain.
